refinement_sections.content: Clearly define the product in question and the specific benchmark for average sales that needs to be exceeded. This includes identifying the time frame for the sales data collection, which is three months, and specifying the exact sales figures or metrics that constitute the benchmark.

Formulate the null hypothesis (H0) as 'The average sales of the new product do not exceed the benchmark.' Conversely, the alternative hypothesis (H1) will be 'The average sales of the new product exceed the benchmark.' Clearly state the significance level (alpha) to be used for the hypothesis test, commonly set at 0.05.

Detail the data collection process from the sales team, ensuring that data is gathered consistently over the specified three-month period. Specify the method of data collection, such as whether it is through sales reports or direct input from sales personnel, and ensure that it captures all relevant sales transactions.

Select an appropriate statistical test for the hypothesis, such as a one-sample t-test if the sales data is normally distributed or a non-parametric test if it is not. Justify the choice of test based on the characteristics of the data.

List and discuss the assumptions made during the analysis, including normality of sales data, independence of observations, and the appropriateness of the chosen statistical test. Explain how violations of these assumptions could affect the validity of the test results.

Outline the steps for conducting the hypothesis test, including data preprocessing, conducting the test, calculating the p-value, and making a decision regarding the null hypothesis based on the p-value and the significance level.

Provide a clear structure for the report that includes an introduction to the problem, methodology, results of the hypothesis test, interpretation of findings, and actionable recommendations based on the results. Ensure that visual aids such as graphs or tables are included to enhance understanding.

Discuss the limitations of the current analysis, such as sample size or external market factors that could influence sales. Suggest areas for future research to further assess market performance or to refine the product offering.
